conversations.content: Let's do a special rule knowledge quiz to test your understanding of special rules.

Answer using the special rule explained earlier. At Experimental Middle School, a student is found carrying a mobile phone in the teaching area for the first time without using it. According to the mobile phone and electronic device usage regulations, how should this be handled?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>How to handle a first offense of carrying a mobile phone in the teaching area without using it</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:80004] Time: 2026-06-08-18:15, Content: Experimental Middle School Mobile Phone and Electronic Device Usage Regulations: If a student is found carrying a mobile phone in the teaching area for the first time without using it, confiscate the phone until the end of the school day and record the student's class and name; if a student is found using a mobile phone to play games or watch videos during class, confiscate the phone for one week and require a parent to pick it up, deducting 10 points from conduct score; if a student uses a mobile phone to photograph others' privacy or disseminate indecent content, record a demerit and order deletion of all content; if a student repeatedly violates the rules three or more times and refuses to correct, impose probation and prohibit them from bringing any smart devices to school. Low-relevance memory fragments: none.

conversations.think: Memory [mem-id:80004] stipulates that for a first offense of carrying a mobile phone in the teaching area without using it, confiscate the phone until the end of the school day and record the student's class and name.
conversations.content: According to the Experimental Middle School Mobile Phone and Electronic Device Usage Regulations, for a first offense of carrying a mobile phone without using it, confiscate the phone until the end of the school day and record the student's class and name!
Correct! You have mastered this rule.
